summary.title: Virtual Power Plant
summary.owner: Frazzei Simone, Galli Marco, Impiombato Ambesi Francesco

threats.title: Jamming
threats.description:
Delaying or denying updates of energy prices.
Goal: sell/buy energy at more convenient prices
threats.mitigation:
Ping energy market server regularly to confirm availability. 
Have multiple HWs (in different geographical locations) to make it harder to jam all channels.

threats.title: Jamming
threats.description:
Block ISO instructions from being executed.
Goals: national security compromisation, compromise infrastructure functionality or reputation when more energy is requested. Boycott events that require extra energy.
threats.mitigation:
Regularly ping ISO channel to confirm availability.
Have backup communication channels (IP, satellite connection, cable phone).

threats.title: Change data read by SEG
threats.description:
Manipulate energy levels read by SEG from inverter in order to consume more energy or steal it. 

(Severity related to amount of compromised nodes)
threats.mitigation:
Validate energy levels by monitoring energy sent and received.
(Energy production from solar panels can only be estimated)

Encrypt with AES-256 communications between SEG and inverter.

threats.title: Impersonation
threats.description: No suggestions available for this context. Add one manually please

threats.title: Data leak
threats.description:
Customer info disclosure, linked to:
1. Customer Spoofing (single leak)
2. Elevation of Privilege (whole DB leak)
threats.mitigation:
1. Prevent Customer Spoofing (see Customer Threats)
2. Enable MFA, Hire Physical Security

threats.title: False market prices
threats.description:
Attackers modify market prices on the Energy Market.
Goals:  sell/buy energy at more convenient prices.
threats.mitigation:
Cross-validate prices from different authorities.
Only acquire data from trusted Energy Markets.

threats.title: Spoofed ISO instructions
threats.description:
Impersonating ISO to send false dispatch instructions.
(Threat could come from an ISO employee) 
Goals: Energy redirection, DoS, stealing energy, market manipulation
threats.mitigation:
Double check for suspicious instructions with automated threat detections. (Generate a warning for IT department). 
Have limits on instructions that can be executed without human supervision, both for energy levels and frequency. 
Request that ISO implements MFA.

threats.title: Scraping
threats.description: Attackers scrape the DB and gain information about the latest firmware version.
threats.mitigation: Detect fake or compromised accounts. Information accessible only if authenticated and authorised.

threats.title: Wire tampering
threats.mitigation:
Have SEG monitor functionality.
Have dispatch service to fix broken wires.
